commands.repair_reason: Protected maintainer labeling plus the native Windows proof gap and compatibility semantics make this a maintainer/contributor validation path, not an automated repair job.; Cleared: The diff narrows a shell-exec path on Windows and adds test/changelog coverage without adding dependencies, CI changes, secrets handling, or new execution surfaces. (sha=fde7096e11e8b21dc474d1f9ceac3ad330432e33)
commands.review_summary:
This PR makes the optional shell-env fallback return an empty successful probe on win32, threads a platform override into the fallback path, adds regression coverage, and adds a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. for a high-confidence source path: current main config loading can call loadShellEnvFallback, and the probe resolves and executes the login-shell path without a win32 guard when expected keys are missing. I did not run a native Windows failing smoke in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Protected maintainer labeling plus the native Windows proof gap and compatibility semantics make this a maintainer/contributor validation path, not an automated repair job.
